FIXING OF RTDs/THERMO COUPLES:
SCOPE:
This procedure details the recommended practice for bonding of RTDs
and/or thermocouples into Thrust Pads, Journal Pads and Bushes.
The adhesives is a thermally conductive, one part room temperature
Activator-curing adhesive system designed for bonding heat generating
components and heat sinks. The high thermal conductivity provides
excellent heat dissipation for thermally sensitive components while the
controlled strength permits field and service repairs.
Consumables:
Loctite(R) product OutputTM 384 (comprising of Output 384 Adhesive &
Output 7387 Activator).
The recommended adhesive thickness is 0.25 mm, therefore the thermo
hole should be drilled to a diameter 0.5mm greater than the probe tip
diameter.
DIRECTION FOR USE:
Refer to the following COSHH Assessment sheets for Health & Safety
Data on:
Adhesive: CA1254
Activator: CA1255
Ensure surfaces are cleaned prior to bonding to remove any dirt, grease,
swarf etc. Use applicator provided to apply the activator into the thermo
Triveni Turbines Ltd
chunks.source_file: USER MANNUAL JUNG SEOK ENGINEERING C-3421
chunks.source_path: D:\기계도면\기계도면\32. 증기터빈 (정석)\제주 VP\기계\16. 운전 및 유지관리 지침서\USER MANNUAL JUNG SEOK ENGINEERING C-3421.pdf

chunks.text:
applicator provided to apply the activator into the thermo
Triveni Turbines Ltd OVERHAUL AND INSPECTION O14
hole in the pad or bush. After the solvent evaporates, the active
ingredients will appear wet, and will remain active up to 2 hours after
application.
Contamination of the surface before bonding should be prevented.
Apply adhesive onto the probe tip of the RTD or thermocouple. The
amount of adhesive applied should be sufficient to form a small fillet where
the leads exit the pad or bush.
The dispensing or application of the adhesive should be done in such a
manner as to minimize air entrapment within the thermo hole.
Insert the probe into the thermo hole and secure until adhesive has fixed
(approximately 5 minutes) before any further handling.
FULL CURE OCCURS IN 4-24 HOURS.
DEVICE REMOVAL/REPAIR:
Since adhesive bond strength decreases at approximately 65-93oC (150-
200oF), devices bonded with this adhesive can be removed while hot
using heat from a hot air jet or by immersion in a hot-oil bath.
Triveni Turbines Ltd

VIBRATIONS:
If vibration amplitudes become too high or if unusual noises occur, the plant must
be shut down immediately. The first measures to be taken are to check balance,
the alignment, bearings, labyrinths, blading and exact clearances at all locations.
Before pre-commissioning check whether the rotor can be turned by hand wheel
of the turning gear. If the vibration level is still dangerously high after pre-
commissioning, an exact vibration investigation must be carried out in order to
find the cause.
High vibrations can result from one or more of the following causes:
 Severe vibration, caused by sudden appearance of imbalance e.g. blade
failure, or a gradual change of state of balance e.g. resulting from
deposits, erosion, corrosion, rotor deformation
 Rotor (blades or labyrinths) rubbing on casing
 Misalignment (Pedestal-Casing or Casing-Gear Box / Concentricity of
bearing and labyrinth bore on as per specification
 Resonances in foundation or in individual machine components
 Induced vibrations from coupling or alignment errors as well as reaction
forces in the driving or driven machines
 Self-excited rotor vibrations
 Pipe line strains coming on turbine
 Lesser labyrinth clearance
 Improper Heating
Triveni Turbines Ltd

DEPOSITS IN STEAM TURBINES:
Detection of deposits
The internal condition of a turbine with respect to mineral deposits should be
continuously monitored during operation. This monitoring is possible only
indirectly by steam data.
Pressure Monitoring
The simplest form of detection is measuring of the pressure of the expanding
steam, such as in the wheel chamber and if possible at further significant stages
in the blading.
After first commissioning the user makes an actual measurement curve. In the
course of further operation the user measures these steam pressures and
compares them with the original values taken with a clean turbine. If comparison
indicates increase in pressure with same operating conditions, this indicates
formation of deposits. Upstream of a reduced flow section the pressure
increases, and downstream it decreases compared with the normal values. If in
the steam flow range of 70- 100% (it is always preferable to compare measured
values in this range) the wheel case / inner casing pressure increases by more
than 5-10%, in spite of that stated above, can also lead to reduced operational
reliability :-
The reasons are,
a) As stated, the aggressiveness of the deposits plays a decisive role in
operational reliability. An increase in the measured wheel case pressure
shows only a reduction in the flow section.
Triveni Turbines Ltd

chunks.text:
l case pressure
shows only a reduction in the flow section.
Triveni Turbines Ltd OVERHAUL AND INSPECTION O28
b) When judging the amount of deposits on blading it must be observed that
on the front stages an immediate pressure rise is caused. With deposits
on the subsequent stages hardly any rise is caused in the wheel case
pressure. In this case a temperature rise in the exhaust provides better
indication.
Pressure Characteristis Index.
Exact monitoring of the pressure in the turbine is possible by means of
determining and comparing the pressure characteristic index, as herewith the
corresponding exhaust pressures and their influence on the pressure expansion
in the turbine is taken into consideration. Pressure in turbine, exhaust pressure
and steam flow vary independent of temperature in accordance with the cone
equation of stodala. The pertaining equation suited to the turbine conditions is:-
C = (P 2 - P 2): D2
1 2
Where:-
P =pressure upstream of the range considered in bar.

Where:-
P =pressure upstream of the range considered in bar. P =pressure downstream of the range considered in bar. D = Steam flow in kg/sec.
Especially with backpressure turbines this equation is more precise than the
simple pressure monitoring. The pressure characteristic C for the new plant is
arrived at from the readings taken by the user after first commissioning. For
monitoring during operation the user takes measurements under conditions as
near as practicable to the original measuring.
From these reading ratios C is calculated and then compared to the C value of
the new plant. Position of control valves (steam flow) must also be same.
Progress of increasing thickness of deposits as a function of time can be
determined if C values measured at various time are plotted against time in a
graph.

Triveni Turbines Ltd OVERHAUL AND INSPECTION O29
After initial commissioning the user plots an exhaust temperature graph, i.e, the
exhaust temperature measured as accurately as possible is plotted against
steam flow. When the initial actual value graph is plotted, the inlet steam
conditions should be measured and recorded as accurately as possible. These
values should be repeated as precisely as practicable for subsequent
measurement comparison.
Increased exhaust temperatures under the same conditions indicate formation of
deposits. If the exhaust temperature should rise by more than 10% with steam
flow between 70 – 100%, than the turbine operating reliability is reduced.
Precaution:-
a). Contact Triveni Customer Care Cell for advice
c) Improve steam quality
d) Clean Turbine
Intervals for Checking
The intervals should fit in with the works operational conditions.
Triveni Turbine Ltd. Make the following recommendations
i) Pressure Monitoring = Daily
ii) Temperature Monitoring = Daily
iii) Pressure characteristic index = Weekly
iv) Specific steam consumption = Monthly
Removing of Deposits:-
Water-soluble deposits may be washed off with condensate or wet steam. Water
in-soluble deposits must be removed mechanically after dismantling the turbine.
The known chemical cleaning methods of turbines for water-insoluble deposits
not recommended because of the involved considerable risks. Turbines which
are frequently started are less liable to form deposits on the blades. This is an
Triveni Turbines Ltd
chunks.source_file: USER MANNUAL JUNG SEOK ENGINEERING C-3421
chunks.source_path: D:\기계도면\기계도면\32. 증기터빈 (정석)\제주 VP\기계\16. 운전 및 유지관리 지침서\USER MANNUAL JUNG SEOK ENGINEERING C-3421.pdf

chunks.text:
are less liable to form deposits on the blades. This is an
Triveni Turbines Ltd OVERHAUL AND INSPECTION O30
advantageous consequence of the so called self cleaning effect. Possible
deposits are cleansed off when the turbine is started from the cold due to the
forming condensate. However, this does not apply for silica deposits.
From experience it can be recommended to start with condensate or wet steam
cleaning process. Very often water soluble combinations are embedded in water-
insoluble substances.
After washing out the soluble portion, the remaining skeleton is expected to
collapse and to be cleansed away. The following stated information to the various
cleaning procedures deals only with the features. Proper cleaning instructions
are not provided in this document.
WASHING WITH CONDENSATE
The turbine cooled down to or heated up to 100 oC is filled with condensate of
100 oC via a turbine drain. A hose level gauge connected to a second drain
serves for checking the condensate level in the turbine. On running barring gear
or rotor turning by hand, respectively, the condensate remains 2 to 4 hours in the
turbine and is then drained. According to test result of the drained condensate
the washing process is repeated. On the first filling the steam turbine should be
filled up to the top. For further fillings condensate levels up to the rotor center
generally will sufficient.
After the washing process the steam turbine is started or is dried with the aid od
back- pressure steam. Condensers that are upended on springs must be
propped prior to filling with condensate. The difference between cylinder and
condensate temperature shall not exceed 40 oC condensate must not enter the
lives steam line.
WASHING WITH WET STEAM
Wet steam is generally produced by injection of condensate with back-pressure
turbines. The exhaust steam is led through a gate valve into open air. For
condensing turbines the vacuum/condensate extraction pump remains out of
Triveni Turbines Ltd

chunks.text:
rbines the vacuum/condensate extraction pump remains out of
Triveni Turbines Ltd OVERHAUL AND INSPECTION O31
service. The cooling water is running so that the entering wet steam is
condensed. The collected condensate is drained from the open turbine drain
valves and disposed to sewerage or as per each country’s environmental
standard. A further is to extract the washing steam condensate from the hot well,
the condensate pump and to lead it via the blanked off discharge nozzle in to the
sewerage or and disposed as above.
Running of washing steam, the turbine is maintained at about 20% of normal
speed, however , with amd adequate safely margin to possible critical speeds to
be observed. By means of a preceding valve the steam is throttled to the effect
that emergency stop valve and control valves are fully opened. The washing
steam conditions is gradually adjusted to a final wetness of X= 0.90 to 0.95
IMPORTANT:-
 Reduce slowly the temperature in the turbine
 Do not change the washing steam temperature by more than1 oC / minute
 Open all turbine cylinder/casing drain. When approaching the saturation
line check steadily and carefully pressure, temperature and if possible also
the washing steam flow rate and the rate of injected condensate.
 Pursue all the values in the mollier-chart.
 Provide sufficient wetness.
 Throttling in longer pipe extensions of gate valve between the point of
injection, the turbine may cause the steam to be finally superheated,
therefore, measure the pressure and temperature just upstream of the
turbine; from the drain upstream and downstream of the steam turbine
condensate must emerge.
 To improve the efficiency of washing it is advantageous to raise gradually
the pressure in the turbine all over the washing process. i.e. by increasing
the speed such keeping cylinder and rotor temperature constantly below
the washing steam temperature, which results in intensified steam
condensation.
 During the washing procedure is in accordance with NEMA TU (SM 20-
4.07)

Triveni Turbines Ltd OVERHAUL AND INSPECTION O32
WASHING WITH CAUSTIC SODA
The washing process with caustic soda involves a risk. If the user considers
carrying out this then only proceed. Triveni Turbine Ltd. does not recommend
this method because of the involved risks. The fluid consists of hot caustic
soda which is diluted by condensate. Any responsibility remains with the applier.
Triveni Turbine Ltd. Basically dissuade from carrying out caustic washing
combined with wet steam because it is almost impossible to receive a perfect
admixture of the washing steam and diluted caustic soda. Caustic
concentrations within the wet steam flow are expected to form, which results in
an increased concentration of the condensate during the process of evaporation.
These caustic concentrations may exist all over the turbine and may cause
damage. Caustic washing combined with wet steam is not allowed.
CLEANING OF DISMANTLED TURBINE
Sediments may be removed with sand blasting equipment. The later should only
be done in the work of the turbine because blasting may damage the blades.
DEPOSITS IN STEAM TURBINES
Monitoring of the washing process
Any turbine should be constantly water analyzed. This will bring about clues
concerning the dissolved quantity and the necessary washing period. In order to
see when the washing can be stopped, measuring the conductivity of the
draining water would suffice. The samples should be taken at equal intervals
during steady washing. If the conductivity approximates that of the entering
washing steam the washing process may be stopped.
The measured values during the process should be plotted against the time
preferably using the double logarithmic scale. Applying this kind of representation
the end of the washing period may be early estimated by extrapolation.
Triveni Turbines Ltd

Triveni Turbines Ltd SHUTDOWN & STORAGE P1
SHUTDOWN & STORAGE
I. PROLONGED SHUTDOWN
a. Preservation of Shut down Turbine Plants
b. Short shut-down up to 4 days
c. Shut-down longer than 4 days to 6 months
d. Shut-down of 6 to 8 months
e. Shut-down for indefinite period, longer than 6 to 8 months
II. RUST-PROTECTIVE TECTYL 506, 511
a. TECTYL 506
b. TECTYL 511-M
III. JOBSITE STORAGE OF GOODS
a. General
b. Recommended Precuations During Storage
Ia. PRESERVATION OF SHUT-DOWN TURBINE PLANTS
GENERAL:
Shut-down the turbine plants are exposed to corrosion. Mostlyan electro-
chemical process is involved in which iron is converted to rust by presence
of water and air (oxygen). Also the most disadvantageous forming may
occur. This process begins immediately after the turbine is shut-down and
has subsequently cooled–down. Air temperatures below dew point cause
condensation of air moisture on internal surfaces. The following measures
were worked out especially for steam turbine plants. The described
measures are to be understood as recommendations and must be
decided upon on site according to actual operating conditions, shut-down
period costs plant location and plant size. For application and execution of
such measures Triveni Turbines Ltd (TTL) personnel should be employed.
Leak steam and condensate penetration to plant sections should be
prevented first of all.
Triveni Turbines Ltd

chunks.text:
tration to plant sections should be
prevented first of all.
Triveni Turbines Ltd SHUTDOWN & STORAGE P2
Ib. SHORT SHUT-DOWN (UP TO 4 DYAS)
The shut-down period counts from complete cooling-down. Special
preventive measures are not required. In turbines furnished with drying
equipment the drying air is switched on after turbine has cooled-down.
The turning gear can be disconnected in case down-period is longer than
72 h. prior to a restart, check tenability of the turbine rotor and watch
running smoothness at low speed.
I c. SHUT-DOWN LONGER THAN 4 DAYS UPTO 6 MONTHS
(plants which must be in operation readiness after hour of preparation)
TURBINE:
Protect turbine against possible entering of leak steam. Do not use the
emergency stop value as a component of steam lock. It is not protected
against corrosion. In turbines with furnished drying equipment keep inner
space of turbine dry by blowing air, the atmospheric humidity of which is
less than that of the air in the machine hall (less than 60%) or by lowing in
pre-warmed air to the effect that casing and rotor temperatures are kept
above dew and freezing point. The air supply is connected to the turbine
exhaust nozzle. Drains are used for air exhaust.
Start the turning gear only shortly before restart and watch running
smoothness.
PRESERVATION OF SHUT-DOWN TURBINE PLANTS
OIL SYSTEM:
Every 2 weeks put oil pump for 1 hour in operation. During this period all
control devices shall be moved once from COMPLETELY CLOSED to
COMPLETELY OPEN. Turn turbine rotor manually for approx. 2 turns;
record in the turbine which provides good protection against rusting after
the above prescribed period before putting into operation.
Triveni Turbines Ltd

Take oil samples as accustomed and drain, if applicable, accumulated
condensate from the oil tank.
Id. SHUTDOWN 6 TO 8 MONTHS
The preventive measures described in this section are applied usually with
seasonally operated plants.
TURBINE:
Secure against entering of leak steam by closing the main stream isolation
valve.
For internal preservation of the turbine, select one of the following
described 4 procedures:
 Regular turbine overhaul by complete dismantling is planned to such
cases preservation of all internal components with VALVOLINE
TECHNICAL 511 M is recommended.
 Keeping the turbine dry by blowing in dry air or pre-warmed air as follows:
Keep the inner space of the turbine dry by induction of air, the
atmospheric humidity of which is less than that of the air in the machine
than 60%) or by the induction of pre-warmed air to the effect that casing
and rotor temperatures are kept in excess of dew point.
The air supply is connected to the turbine exhaust nozzle. The wheel
chamber drain serves for air exhaust. If there is a vent or a drain between
emergency stop and control valves, then these components can be used
for an exhaust. Prior to a restart check rotation of the turbine rotor and
warm running smoothness at low speed.
PRESERVATION OF SHUT-DOWN TURBINE PLANTS
Inductions of a corrosion inhibitor in the steam system just before
shutdown. An oil-steam admixture is produced by means of portable
injection equipment. After the oil-steam admixture is being blown into the
turbine, the oil is expected to form a protective coat on the walls when

Triveni Turbines Ltd SHUTDOWN & STORAGE P4
hitting any internal surfaces. For pre-commissioning dismantling is not
required.
Sufficient is 1 to 2 hours steam flushing operation via exhaust relief valve.
(Flushing steam need not to be dry and saturated.)
The so-called nitrogen procedure requires immediate expansion to
atmosphere after shutdown and subsequent air drying. Seal turbine by
carefully closing of all valves; maintain slight nitrogen over pressure of 5 to
10 mm (water column); nitrogen demand depends on the tightness of all
sealing and can be ascertained only by experiment. (For reference,
Nitrogen demand for a 35 MW turbine amounts to approx. 8 m3 /h;) control
valves and emergency stop valves remain open. Check for complete
absence of nitrogen prior to restart to avoid accidents!
Check external coating and recoat if necessary. Apply VALVOLINE
TECTYL 506 to bright external components (prior to pre-commissioning
use paraffin for removal), rub in MOLYKOTE on valve stems.
Remove measuring instruments and electrical equipment, mark carefully
and store wrapped in oil paper.
OIL SYSTEM:
If during shutdown no power for the oil pumps is available, all the
components of the oil system were overhauled after shutdown; the
measures should be taken as follows:
Regular turbine overhaul by complete dismantling is planned. In such
cases preservation of all internal components with VALVOLINE TECTYL
511M is recommended.
After this process, the periodical cut-in of the oil pumps as well as
manually turning of the turbine rotor may be omitted, proved that:
 Oil containing the usual corrosion inhibitors remains in the system.
Triveni Turbines Ltd

Triveni Turbines Ltd SHUTDOWN & STORAGE P5
 Oil lines and oil return lines are filled with oil and are isolated by slip
plates.
PRECOMMISSIONING:
After previous preservation it is not necessary to dismantle the turbine
before pre-commissioning. Washing or flushing is carried out with steam.
In case of condensing turbines it is necessary to wash the condenser too
by operating the turbine for about 1 or 2 hours and draining the
condensate. In a condenser being not washed remainders of the corrosion
inhibitor would deposit on the condenser tubes and as a consequence
impair the heat transfer. The washing process with steam must be carried
out with turning rotor at slow speed. For condensing turbines the exhaust
steam temperature must not exceed 120 Deg C.

To protect turbine plants against corrosion for indefinite periods is rather
extensive and expensive. All measures overall overhaul of the complete
turbo-set after shutdown. All preservation in for 2 years intervals and
renew if required. Prior to pre-commissioning the corrosion inhibitors must
be removed with steam or paraffin.
TURBINE:
Dismantle turbine latest after 4 days and remove rotor; check and clean
internals; mark carefully the components; apply TECYL 506 to all
internals, bearings, joints, auxiliary drives, flange bolts, etc. Bearings and
joint surfaces should be additionally wrapped in oil paper. Apply TECTYL
511M to all inner casing surfaces. Dismantled components shall be
wrapped in foils and stored indoors or stored next to the turbine in cases.
Lift upper turbine casing, bearing cover, valve block by about 100 mm
from the horizontal joint by means of hardwood distance pieces.
Triveni Turbines Ltd

visible for everybody.
Clean and apply TECTYL 506 to all dismantled components like steam
strainers, control and emergency stop valves, valve chest, control
components, barring gear etc.
Apply TECTYL 506 and preserve base plates, sliding surfaces, keys,
guide pins. Remove measuring instruments and electrical equipment and
store them
carefully marked and wrapped in oil paper. Check external coatings,
renew if required. Remove piping and clean and dry the individual
sections. Seal air-tight and apply a new external coasting. Order and keep
on store one set of new sealing rings and gaskets for pre-commissioning.
OIL UNIT:
The individual components are wrapped in foils and stored after cleaning,
overhaul and preservation.
If. RE-STARTING AFTER LONG SHUTDOWN:
After preservation it is necessary to dismantle the turbine before re-
starting.
NOTE:- It is important to consult Triveni service persons before
opening the turbine.

II. RUST-PROTECTIVE (TECTYL 506, 511 )
Drain turbine oil and clean; take oil and if require provide a new external
coating; apply TECTYL 511M internally above oil level. Due to the
corrosion inhibitors usually contained in the oil refill the oil tank so that
preservation o the bottom part in the oil tank is provided. Remove piping
fill with oil seal and coat. Clean and overhaul the oil pumps and apply
TECTYL 511 M internally and apply TECTYCL 506 EXTERNALLY; oil
pumps or store them indoors; clean immersion pumps and reinstall.
Clean and dry the oil cooler on the oil and on the water side. On the oil
side apply TECTYL 511M or refill with oil. If scaling off is expected in dry
condition. Renew the internal coating on the water side.
Oil filters; remove inserts, clean and apply TECTYL 511 M or fill oil and
seal.
Remove and clean fittings, valves pressure switch, measuring gauges,
etc.; wrap all equipment in oilpaper.
Triveni Turbines Ltd

RUST PROTECTIVE - TECTYL 506
a. RUST PROTECTIVE - TECTYL 506
PPOPERTIES:
TECTYL506 is a rust-preventive especially suitable for export of machines
of various types. When being supplied, product is liquid. The resulting wax
film is not affecting any metal surfaces and does not attack lacquer,
plastics and rubber materials. This brown rust preventive is resistant
against temperature occurring in packing, which amount to 70-90 0C in
some countries. The wax film
furthermore has an outstanding adhesion with minus temperatures.
TECTYL 506 with its flexible protective film being tight and adhesive forms
a good barrier between metal surface to be protected and corrosive
influences. Coverage amounts to 6-8 m2 per liter-depending on the film
thickness.
Protection period on clean steel surfaces
(Film thickness approx.100 Microns):
- Inside storage* 4-5years
- Outside storage* 18 months to years
APPLICATION:
TECTYL 506 can be applied by painting or spraying or airless to the parts
to be protected.
SPECIFICATION:
TECTYL 506 fulfills US-Military
Specification MIL–C-16 173 D, Grade4
Triveni Turbines Ltd

II b. RUST PROTECTIVE - TECTYL 511-M
PROPERTIES:
TECTYL 511-M is a fluid sloshing liquid. The resulting film is light-brown,
nearly transparent and soft.
Coverage amounts to 35-45 m3 per Liter. TECTYL 511-M is an especially
water displacing and creepable product. The film does not attack any
metal surfaces. This product is very suitable for temporary storage and
sort time dispatch (metals being protected by TECTYL 511-M must be
covered o that neither rain water nor sunshine can affect them). It has a
good separating effect between metals and aggressive mediums so that
steel is protected against rust and aluminum-magnesium-alloys against
efflorescence.
Protection period on clean steel surfaces
(Film thickness approx. 15 Microns):
-Inside storage * approx.18monts
-Outside storage*
Triveni Turbines Ltd

III. JOBSITE STORAGE OF GOODS
a. GENERAL
During storage before and during erection and first start certain
precautions must be taken in order to prevent corrosion damage to the
plant equipment.
This instruction primarily covers the equipment delivered by Triveni
Turbines Ltd (TTL), Bangalore. All goods are packed for approximately
two-three months storage (including transportation) when leaving Triveni
Turbines Ltd works at Bangalore. Special Attention must be paid to the
generator (see generator manual). If the relative humidity of the
environment is higher than 50%, the recommended precautions must be
taken immediately to prevent corrosion damage. If the standstill period is
expected to exceed 9-12 months, or if the environment is extremely
demanding, more extensive conservation procedure should be
considered.
b. RECOMMENDED PRECUATIONS DURING STORAGE
All machinery, electrical and other sensitive parts shall be stored indoors.
If the storage time exceeds 2-3 months, the packages shall be opened
and a dehumidifier unit connected to the storage room in order to keep
relative humidity below 50%. Other equipments, such as piping and less
sensitive materials, need not to be stored dry indoors, Instead, rust
protection by use of rust preventive agents e.g. Tectyl TM , water- soluble
oils and silica gels inside sealed-off space can be used. Weather
protection and periodic inspections are recommended.
Triveni Turbines Ltd

Triveni Turbines Ltd SHUTDOWN & STORAGE P13 ARRIVAL AT SITE
 GENERAL
Place Crates and boxes so that they will not be exposed to water (rain,
flooding etc.) and /or physical damage.
Do not open any box or crate until the equipment in the specific box or
crate is to be used. If an opened box or crate is to be stored further, it
must be carefully closed and resealed. At the opening of the different
boxes an inventory must take place using the packing list for each box, in
the presence of the TTL Site Representative
 SPARE PARTS
Spare Parts are to be placed indoors in a ventilated space with normal
room temperature and preferably low humidity. Rust inhibitors should not
be removed until each part is intended to be used.
 CONTROL CUBICLES
Do not remove or damage the plastic film that covers each cubicle. If an
inspection of the cubicles has to be made, the moisture absorbent (silica
gel) must not be removed from inside the cubicles. Welding as soon as
possible should then hermetically reseal the plastic film.
Do not place the cubicles in their final space until the conditions are such
that no condensation will take place inside the cubicles.
Triveni Turbines Ltd
